title: pelargonium × knappianum
intro: Pelargonium × knappianum, also known as the Red Form or Mountain Rose, is a hybrid of the Pelargonium tomentosum and Pelargonium cucullatum species of plants in the Geraniaceae family. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × knappianum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of the common cold, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
